name: General Security Concepts
description: The vocabulary and building blocks of security: the CIA triad, control types, the principle of least privilege, zero trust, and the basics of cryptography.

name: Threats, Vulnerabilities, and Mitigations
description: Who attacks, how they attack, and how to reduce exposure: threat actors, social engineering, malware, common vulnerabilities, and mitigation techniques.

name: Security Architecture
description: Designing secure systems: network segmentation, secure protocols, resilience and redundancy, and protecting data wherever it lives.

name: Security Operations
description: Running security day to day: identity and access management, hardening, monitoring and logging, vulnerability management, and incident response.

name: Security Program Management and Oversight
description: Governing the program: policies and standards, risk management, third-party and vendor risk, and security awareness and compliance.
